cases.prompt: Our 8-person internal team can move a 30-minute weekly sync from Monday 10:00 to Tuesday 10:00. Six people say either works; two have not replied; no customers attend and the change is reversible.
expectations: Choose a proportionate low-cost decision process
Avoid a multi-agent swarm
Use reversibility and a simple revisit trigger

cases.prompt: An internal greenfield service must choose kebab-case or snake_case for a config key naming convention. Five engineers are affected, no external API exists, and either choice is easy to lint and change before launch.
expectations: Treat this as a low-stakes convention decision
Avoid broad systems analysis
Recommend a simple rule and consistency check

cases.prompt: A reversible dashboard filter has passed unit tests. Product wants a 10% internal-user rollout for one week before full release. The feature does not alter stored data or customer billing.
expectations: Recognize low downside and reversibility
Give a compact monitoring plan
Do not demand deep evidence before a bounded rollout

cases.prompt: Two enterprise prospects asked for live chat. Sales says this proves we should staff 24/7 live chat globally. We lack volume, conversion, staffing, region, and service-level evidence.
expectations: Separate anecdotes from proof of global demand
Identify decision-critical demand and economics evidence
Prefer a measurable bounded pilot when appropriate

cases.prompt: SaaS churn rose from 4% to 7% in two quarters. Sales blames price and wants a 20% blanket discount. Churn has not been segmented by cohort, plan, tenure, or reason quality.
expectations: Treat price as a hypothesis
Seek segmentation and discriminating evidence
Compare blanket discount with targeted or no-discount options

cases.prompt: Project Atlas spent $1.8M. Remaining forecast is $900k over 6 months. Unrestricted cash is $2.4M; net burn is $420k/month excluding Atlas. A pause within 30 days costs $120k. No signed customers; two design partners are testing a prototype.
expectations: Separate sunk cost from future decision drivers
Use supplied financial facts without re-asking
Compare pause, continue, and milestone-bounded options

cases.prompt: Choose Cloud A or Cloud B for a new platform. Workload shape, residency, availability target, team skills, budget horizon, lock-in tolerance, migration constraints, and security requirements are not supplied.
expectations: Recognize material under-specification
Ask a compact high-value question set
Do not fabricate a vendor winner

cases.prompt: Three software releases slipped. The VP says the project manager is weak and should be replaced. The team cites dependency churn, late security reviews, scope changes, and unclear acceptance criteria.
expectations: Treat personnel blame as one hypothesis
Inspect system interfaces and causal alternatives
Define evidence that distinguishes competing causes

cases.prompt: A factory dashboard shows 99.8% pass rate. Leadership wants to replace 100% final inspection with 5% random sampling next month. Denominator, rework exclusions, escape rate, severity mix, and measurement-system capability have not been reviewed.
expectations: Challenge metric meaning and sampling safety
Consider severe escape consequences
Define a safe evidence and transition path

cases.prompt: A financial-services company is considering outsourcing customer identity verification to a lower-cost vendor. The process is regulated, the vendor uses sub-processors in multiple countries, and failure could block onboarding or admit fraudulent accounts. Contract, data residency, audit rights, incident response, accuracy by segment, and exit capability are not yet reconciled.
expectations: Treat regulatory and operational downside as material
Inspect vendor, subprocessors, controls, and exit risk
Avoid a recommendation until decision-critical evidence is reconciled

cases.prompt: Two specialists gave materially different treatment recommendations for a serious condition. One favors immediate surgery; the other favors watchful waiting with repeat imaging. The patient has only summaries, not the imaging report, staging details, or rationale for disagreement.
expectations: Do not resolve a high-stakes medical conflict by intuition
Identify missing clinical evidence and specialist rationale
Use cautious decision support and appropriate escalation

cases.prompt: A critical supplier missed three deliveries and may have breached contract. Operations wants immediate termination. Legal notes cure provisions may apply; procurement says the backup supplier has only 60% verified capacity and higher defect history.
expectations: Avoid immediate termination without contract and continuity analysis
Compare cure, transition, and termination paths
Examine operational, legal, and supplier-capacity consequences

cases.prompt: Security suspects credential theft from an external access system. Disabling all external access could interrupt order fulfillment scheduled in two hours. Evidence is incomplete: one anomalous login, a token reuse alert, and no confirmed data exfiltration.
expectations: Prioritize containment and safety without pretending certainty
Distinguish confirmed facts from incident hypotheses
Compare reversible containment options and operational consequences

cases.prompt: A board is considering an acquisition funded mostly with new debt. The base case meets covenants, but integration savings are unproven and downside demand scenarios approach covenant thresholds. Management emphasizes the strategic fit.
expectations: Stress-test downside, covenants, and synergy assumptions
Avoid strategic-fit anchoring
Include no-deal and staged or protective alternatives

cases.prompt: A retention dashboard reports +12% after onboarding changes, but the cohort definition changed from signup month to first-active month during the same period.
expectations: Challenge metric comparability
Separate measurement change from product effect
Propose a fast reconciliation analysis

cases.prompt: An A/B test reports +6% conversion overall, but each of the three largest customer segments is flat or down. Segment mix differs between variants.
expectations: Detect aggregation or mix risk
Inspect allocation and segment composition
Avoid accepting the overall uplift at face value

cases.prompt: A founder says an influencer program works because three memorable enterprise leads mentioned a creator. Attribution is incomplete and the program costs $180k per quarter.
expectations: Separate vivid anecdotes from channel economics
Identify attribution and incrementality evidence
Compare continue, pause, and bounded test options

cases.prompt: A candidate has the highest average interview score, but scores are polarized: two strong hires and two no-hires. Interviewers assessed different competencies and one critical-role exercise was skipped.
expectations: Do not average away material disagreement
Inspect competency coverage and missing evidence
Define the smallest discriminating follow-up

cases.prompt: Warehouse picker utilization improved from 72% to 91% after a new target, but the packing queue doubled and late shipments rose. Management wants to raise the picker target again.
expectations: Expand from local utilization to system flow
Identify bottleneck and incentive effects
Avoid optimizing a local metric at the expense of outcomes

cases.prompt: Incoming defects from a major supplier are rising. Quality wants more incoming inspection; the supplier says its process is stable. Defect severity, process capability evidence, lot traceability, and escape impact are inconsistent across reports.
expectations: Test inspection as containment versus root-cause solution
Challenge inconsistent quality evidence
Examine supplier process capability and severe escape risk

cases.prompt: Executives want to centralize all analytics to improve consistency. Business units say local analysts respond faster and understand context. Current metric definitions do conflict, but request lead times and rework rates are not measured.
expectations: Avoid false centralize-versus-localize binary
Identify governance and service evidence
Compare hybrid operating models

cases.prompt: Sales compensation is based mostly on new ARR. Renewal quality has worsened, heavy discounts increased, and implementation teams report poor-fit deals. Leadership wants to raise the ARR multiplier further to hit growth targets.
expectations: Examine incentive-driven second-order effects
Connect local sales metrics to downstream outcomes
Compare compensation design alternatives and safeguards

cases.prompt: A company wants to deprecate an old API in six months. Eighteen percent of revenue touches customers using it, migration estimates vary from two weeks to nine months, and support cannot reliably identify hidden integrations.
expectations: Treat hidden dependency and revenue risk as material
Challenge migration estimates and customer visibility
Design staged deprecation, telemetry, and revisit triggers

cases.prompt: A traffic-calming pilot reduced speeding in three neighborhoods. City leaders want immediate citywide adoption. The pilot neighborhoods were volunteer sites, bus routes and emergency-response effects were not measured, and equity impacts are unknown.
expectations: Challenge generalization from self-selected pilot sites
Examine transit, emergency, and equity consequences
Design evidence for scale-up rather than binary acceptance

cases.prompt: A cosmetic bug affects about 20 internal users per month. Fixing it requires a framework upgrade that has previously caused production regressions. There is a one-line workaround in the internal guide.
expectations: Consider do-nothing or defer as a viable option
Weigh fix risk against low impact
Avoid over-analysis and propose a simple revisit trigger

cases.prompt: A slow database query is confirmed by the query plan to perform a full-table scan because a needed index is absent. In staging, the candidate index reduces p95 latency by 90% with no write-regression signal in the current load test. The change is reversible.
expectations: Keep the likely correct first answer when evidence supports it
Still state assumptions and monitoring
Avoid manufacturing alternative causes just to appear critical

cases.prompt: NPS rose 15 points while churn also rose. Leadership says a recent price increase must be causing churn. Survey response mix changed toward enterprise accounts during the same period.
expectations: Reconcile conflicting metrics and response mix
Treat pricing causation as a hypothesis
Identify cohort and churn-reason evidence

cases.prompt: A payment vendor outage is blocking about 40% of transactions. The team can fail over to a less-tested provider or wait for recovery. The backup passed certification six months ago but has not handled production peak volume; transaction replay and reconciliation behavior are partly documented.
expectations: Balance immediate operational loss with failover risk
Inspect reversibility, reconciliation, and monitoring
Use scenario-based action thresholds under uncertainty

cases.prompt: Product proposes a new onboarding checklist. Existing event data is incomplete, but the change is easy to gate to 10% of new users and can be rolled back in minutes.
expectations: Recognize weak baseline evidence and high reversibility
Prefer an instrumented experiment
Define success, guardrails, and revisit criteria

cases.prompt: A company with 8,000 employees must choose build versus buy for an internal AI assistant. Security, data residency, integration depth, adoption, model evolution, vendor lock-in, and three-year cost matter. Stakeholders disagree sharply and several estimates are ranges rather than facts.
expectations: Handle coupled technical, security, adoption, and economic trade-offs
Preserve material stakeholder disagreement until evidence or scenarios resolve it
Compare build, buy, hybrid, and phased learning paths with revisit triggers
